message:
revert(lexical/cass-compat): roll back r112 "Prefix Direct-Term Stream" experiment; prefix fields return to hyphen_normalize analyzer

The r112 experiment (prior commit
`perf(lexical/cass-compat): split prefix field into direct pre-tokenized
terms using cass_prefix_terms analyzer`) failed to improve ingest
throughput in the real-corpus benchmark harness once tiebreak rounds
r111..r112 were run — the documented rejection is captured in the
sibling `coding_agent_session_search` repo's benchmark log.

Roll the prefix side back to the pre-r112 shape:
- `CASS_SCHEMA_HASH` returns to
  `tantivy-schema-v7-hyphen-cjk-bigrams-prefix-freqs-preview-stored`
  so downstream consumers rebuild off the old, known-good prefix index
  layout instead of the wasted `-prefix-direct` hash.
- `build_cass_tantivy_document` + `build_cass_tantivy_document_ref`
  go back to `cass_generate_edge_ngrams` for `title_prefix` (+ the
  existing `cass_build_content_prefix_and_preview` split for
  `content_prefix`).
- Drop the `WhitespaceTokenizer` import that the rejected path
  required.

Keep the `cass_generate_indexable_prefix_terms` helper and its
support code in place for now — the rejected experiment is fully
documented and may be revisited under different conditions; deleting
the helper would force a second archaeology pass later.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
